banner: Ground Truth expanded · Red Thread narratives · ARIA Intelligence Briefing — now bilingual EN/ES

story.methodologyP1: This analysis was produced by the RUBLI platform, which processes
story.methodologyP2: federal contracts registered in COMPRANET between 2002 and 2025.
story.methodologyP3: Risk scores are generated by model v6.5 (AUC 0.828, vendor-stratified split), trained on 748 documented corruption cases and 603 linked vendors. Scores measure similarity to documented corruption patterns — they do not constitute proof of wrongdoing.
story.methodologyP4: All figures come from public COMPRANET data. Direct award calculations are based on the procedure_type field of the official record. Sector classifications use RUBLI's 12-sector taxonomy based on budget branch codes.
story.notFoundDetail: The story "{{slug}}" could not be found.

welcome.subtitle: Red Unificada de Busqueda de Licitaciones Irregulares — AI-Powered Corruption Detection for Mexican Government Procurement
welcome.step1Desc: 23 years of Mexican federal procurement — 3.1 million contracts, 9+ trillion MXN — every one scored for corruption risk patterns using a model validated against ~748 documented fraud cases (~603 vendors).
welcome.step2Desc: Scores are STATISTICAL INDICATORS (0–1 scale), not verdicts. A score of 0.68 means this contract closely resembles procurement patterns found in documented fraud cases — not that it is definitely corrupt. Correlation, not causation.
welcome.step2Caveat: High score = strong similarity to documented fraud patterns. Investigation triage, not proof.
welcome.step3Desc: Press to open the command palette — search vendors, cases, institutions, or contracts by name. On mobile, tap the search icon in the header.
welcome.step4Desc: The investigation workflow is three steps: find a suspicious vendor, explore their risk profile, then add them to a dossier for export and sharing.

validation.evidenceMinLength: Evidence description must be at least 50 characters

aboutData: Risk scores are computed by a machine learning model trained on 748 documented corruption cases in Mexican federal procurement (2002–2025). Scores measure statistical similarity to known cases — they are not legal determinations or proof of wrongdoing. Source: COMPRANET (SHCP). Methodology: rubli.mx/methodology
